creator: rkoberman@gmail.com
text:
For reference, the HD5500 is a Broadwell chip.

You have had some good advice. Move your configuration file aside. They generally cause trouble other than short files to add fonts or to specify non-default options.

Second, as suggested, remove any xf86-video-intel driver and let the system use the modesetting driver. My older generation Intel graphics worked much better and was faster with modesetting.

Finally, don't try running X as root! This has been considered a security problem for many years. It may be better these days, but I would still not ever do this, especially when I see the xauth error. there was another bug about this issue about a month ago, but I don't recall the resolution. You might search bugzilla.
